title: Productive capacity and net investment
description: Net investment, capital consumption, industrial production, manufacturing, and freight provide different views of productive capacity and material throughput.
plainLanguageSummary: Net investment, capital consumption, industrial production, manufacturing, and freight provide different views of productive capacity and material throughput.
howToRead: Compare direction, turning points, and sustained divergences. Use the tooltip for exact values and observation dates; visual alignment alone does not establish causation.

calculation.formula: Official investment and output series; monthly physical-output indexes are averaged within genuine quarters.
calculation.explanation: Net investment is nominal while capital consumption is real, so raw mode separates units.
calculation.example: A value at date t uses only observations dated t or earlier unless the chart explicitly labels a visual shift.

patternsToWatch: Turning points or divergences that repeat across historical episodes
limitations: Net investment is nominal while capital consumption is real, so raw mode separates units.

methodology.formula: Official investment and output series; monthly physical-output indexes are averaged within genuine quarters.
methodology.notes: Net investment is nominal while capital consumption is real, so raw mode separates units.
